content.text:
Requirements Engineering
Semester:
1  |
Type:
UEF12  |
VHS:
42h  |
Weekly hours:
C: 1h30 / TD: 1h30  |
Personal work VHS:
21h  |
Coefficient:
3  |
Credits:
4
Teaching Objectives
Understand the fundamental concepts of requirements engineering
Discover the different stages of the requirements engineering process: identification, analysis, specification, validation and management
Learn to use the appropriate techniques and tools for each stage
Develop communication and collaboration skills to work effectively with stakeholders
Apply requirements engineering principles in software development projects
Recommended prior knowledge:
Software engineering, UML.
Course Content (Lectures: 21h)
Introduction to Requirements Engineering (RE)
Definition of RE
Role of RE in the software life cycle
Key concepts: needs, functional and non-functional requirements, constraints, etc.
The Requirements Engineering Process
Requirements elicitation: needs gathering techniques
Requirements analysis: needs modelling, conflict management, prioritisation
Requirements specification: writing clear and unambiguous specifications (UML, use cases, etc.)
Requirements validation: review, prototyping, testing
Requirements management: version control, traceability, impact analysis
Techniques and Tools
Needs gathering techniques: interviews, workshops, observations, document analysis
Needs modelling: use case diagrams (UML), class diagrams (UML), data models
Formal specification: specification languages (Alloy, Z)
Requirements management tools: DOORS, RequisitePro, etc.
Requirements Quality
Quality criteria: completeness, consistency, feasibility, etc.
Quality assessment techniques
Requirements quality management
Requirements Engineering in Specific Contexts
Agile development
Embedded systems
Web applications
Complex systems engineering
Assessment
In-person semester exam (60%)
Continuous assessment (CC) (40%): continuous tests, personal work
References
Karl Wiegers, Joy Beatty (2013).
Software Requirements
. Microsoft Press, 3rd ed.
Elizabeth Hull, Ken Jackson, Jeremy Dick (2017).
Requirements Engineering
. Springer, 4th ed.
Klaus Pohl and P.A. Börstler (2010).
Requirements Engineering: Processes and Techniques
.
Stéphane Badreau, Jean-Louis Boulanger (2014).
Ingénierie des exigences
. Dunod.
Martin Fowler (2000).
UML Distilled
.
Sommerville I (2013).
Software Engineering
. Pearson Education.
Roel Wieringa, Anne Persson (2010).
Requirements Engineering: Foundation for Software Quality
. Springer.
